#### Habits citing `heine1834` (depth 2)

- **[legend]** Königsberg townspeople are said to have set their clocks by his walk time.
  - trigger_zone: `peripatos`
  - mechanism: Community-level temporal anchor; ritualised predictability reinforces the agent's own schedule compliance. Flagged as legend by ERRE-Sandbox v0.2 §3.1.

### `jachmann1804` — Reinhold Bernhard Jachmann (1804)

- title: *Immanuel Kant geschildert in Briefen an einen Freund*
- availability: bibliographic_only (citation registered; NO committed document body)

#### Habits citing `jachmann1804` (depth 2)

- **[fact]** Midday dinner 13:00-16:00 with 4-9 invited guests, topic-driven conversation ("minimum Graces, maximum Muses").
  - trigger_zone: `agora`
  - mechanism: Social cognition plus postprandial discourse; the 4-9 cap preserves a small-group discussion regime that sustains deep argumentation.

### `kuehn2001` — Manfred Kuehn (2001)

- title: *Kant: A Biography*
- availability: bibliographic_only (citation registered; NO committed document body)
- body_absent_reason: `in_copyright_license`
- work_pd_by_age: `false` (basis: in copyright (published 2001))
- body_committable: `false`
- metadata_verified: `false`

#### Habits citing `kuehn2001` (depth 2)

- **[fact]** Daily walk on the Linden-Allee at 15:30 (±5 min) for 60-75 minutes; no conversation on the return leg.
  - trigger_zone: `peripatos`
  - mechanism: Rhythmic locomotion activates the Default Mode Network; Oppezzo & Schwartz (2014) report a +60% gain on Guilford's Alternate Uses divergent-thinking task (treadmill and outdoor-loop conditions); the outdoor effect size for sustained 60-minute walks is not directly measured in that study.
- **[legend]** Nasal-only breathing during walks, deliberately suppressing speech.
  - trigger_zone: `peripatos`
  - mechanism: Kant advocates nasal breathing in Anthropologie §44 as generally beneficial, but that his personal practice on walks was nasal-only is biographical tradition (Kuehn 2001 ch. 8) rather than a direct self-report. Hypothesised vagal-tone modulation via nasal respiration remains speculative.
- **[fact]** Morning writing window 05:00-07:00 devoted to long-form critique drafting.
  - trigger_zone: `study`
  - mechanism: Chronotype-aligned prefrontal window supports multi-step deductive reasoning; early-morning low-interference improves working memory.
- **[speculative]** Never travelled more than ~10 miles from Königsberg in his entire life.
  - trigger_zone: `—`
  - mechanism: Local embedded spatial memory may scaffold abstract conceptual navigation (grid-code-on-concept hypothesis, Constantinescu et al. 2016). The original study does not address travel range or philosophical productivity; this linkage is our extrapolation. The behavioural fact (never >10 miles) is well-attested; the cognitive consequence is speculative.
